about.title: About Sea Bridge Logistics
about.missionText: To provide safe, reliable, and efficient shipping solutions connecting Cameroon to the world.
about.visionText: To be the leading logistics partner for businesses across Central and West Africa.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Find answers to common questions about our services.
faq.q1: What services does Sea Bridge Logistics offer?
faq.a1: We offer air, sea, and road freight services, warehousing, customs clearance, and end-to-end supply chain management across Central and West Africa.
faq.q2: How can I track my shipment?
faq.a2: Use our online tracking page at seabridgelogistics.com/track. Enter your tracking number (SBL-XXXX format) to see real-time status updates.
faq.a3: It's our flagship 148km route connecting Cameroon's two major ports — Kribi Deep Sea Port and Douala Port. Transit time is typically 3-5 hours.
faq.a4: Yes, we provide full customs brokerage services to ensure smooth and compliant border crossings for all freight types.
faq.q5: How long does sea freight take?
faq.a5: Transit times vary by destination. Regional shipments within West Africa take 3-7 days. International shipments take 15-45 days depending on the route.
faq.a6: We accept bank transfers, mobile money (MTN MoMo, Orange Money), and cash payments at our offices in Kribi and Douala.
faq.a7: Yes, all shipments can be insured. We recommend cargo insurance for high-value goods. Contact us for a quote.
faq.q8: What are the checkpoints on the Kribi-Douala route?
faq.a8: There are 2 main security checkpoints. Our drivers are fully documented and clearances are handled in advance for minimal delays.

air.title: Air Freight
air.subtitle: Fast, reliable air cargo solutions across Africa and worldwide
air.description: Our air freight service delivers time-sensitive cargo with speed and precision. Whether you're shipping perishables, high-value goods, or urgent commercial shipments, Sea Bridge Logistics provides door-to-door air cargo solutions from Cameroon to destinations worldwide. We partner with major airlines to offer competitive rates and guaranteed transit times.
air.feature3: Door-to-door service from pickup to delivery
air.step1Desc: Tell us your cargo details, origin, and destination. We'll provide a competitive quote within hours.
air.step2Desc: We pick up your cargo from your location in Cameroon or arrange drop-off at our facility.
air.step3Desc: Our team handles all air waybills, export documentation, and customs formalities.
air.step4Desc: Your cargo is loaded and flown via the optimal route. Track progress in real-time.
air.step5Desc: Cargo is cleared at destination and delivered to the final address. You receive confirmation via WhatsApp.
air.faq1Q: What is the maximum weight for air freight?
air.faq1A: There is no strict maximum — we handle everything from small parcels to full charter flights. For shipments over 500kg, we offer bulk rates.
air.faq2Q: How fast is air freight from Cameroon?
air.faq2A: Express air freight to West Africa takes 1-2 days. Shipments to Europe take 2-4 days, and to the Americas or Asia, 3-5 days.
air.faq3Q: Can you ship perishable goods by air?
air.faq3A: Yes, we have temperature-controlled packaging and cold chain logistics for perishables including fresh produce, seafood, and pharmaceuticals.
air.faq4A: Basic carrier liability is included. We strongly recommend additional cargo insurance for high-value shipments — ask us for a quote.

sea.title: Sea Freight
sea.subtitle: Cost-effective ocean freight via Kribi and Douala ports
sea.description: Sea Bridge Logistics provides reliable ocean freight services through Cameroon's two major ports — Kribi Deep Sea Port and Douala Port. We offer both FCL (Full Container Load) and LCL (Less than Container Load) options for imports and exports, with competitive rates and dependable transit schedules to destinations across Africa, Europe, Asia, and the Americas.
sea.feature2: Direct access to Kribi & Douala ports
sea.feature6: Competitive rates with major shipping lines
sea.step1Desc: We evaluate your cargo volume, type, and destination to recommend FCL or LCL options.
sea.step2Desc: We secure vessel space and prepare all shipping documents including bills of lading.
sea.step3Desc: Cargo is packed, sealed, and loaded at our warehouse or your premises.
sea.step4Desc: Your container sails on the scheduled vessel. Track it via our platform.
sea.step5Desc: We handle customs clearance at the destination port and arrange final delivery.
sea.faq1Q: What's the difference between FCL and LCL?
sea.faq1A: FCL means you book an entire container (20ft or 40ft). LCL means your cargo shares a container with other shipments — ideal for smaller volumes.
sea.faq2Q: How long does sea freight take?
sea.faq2A: Regional routes within West Africa take 3-7 days. Europe is 15-25 days, Asia 25-35 days, and the Americas 20-30 days.
sea.faq3Q: Which ports do you operate from?
sea.faq3A: We operate from both Kribi Deep Sea Port and Douala Port, giving you flexibility and access to all major shipping routes.
sea.faq4A: Yes, we offer flat rack containers, open-top containers, and breakbulk solutions for oversized or heavy project cargo.

road.title: Road Freight
road.subtitle: Reliable overland transport across Cameroon and the region
road.description: Our road freight division specializes in the Kribi-Douala corridor and beyond, providing reliable overland transport for all cargo types. With a modern fleet of trucks and experienced drivers, we ensure your goods arrive safely and on time. We also offer cross-border road transport to neighboring countries including Gabon, Equatorial Guinea, Chad, and Nigeria.
road.feature2: Full truckload and partial load options
road.step1Desc: We plan the optimal route based on your cargo type, size, and delivery deadline.
road.step2Desc: The right truck is assigned — standard, flatbed, or refrigerated — based on your needs.
road.step3Desc: Professional loading at your premises or our warehouse with proper securing.
road.step4Desc: GPS tracking with checkpoint updates. You receive WhatsApp notifications at every stage.
road.step5Desc: Cargo delivered with proof of delivery. Digital confirmation sent immediately.
road.faq1Q: How long is the Kribi-Douala route?
road.faq1A: The corridor is 148km and transit takes 3-5 hours depending on traffic and checkpoint processing.
road.faq2Q: Do you offer cross-border road transport?
road.faq2A: Yes, we transport to Gabon, Equatorial Guinea, Chad, Nigeria, and other CEMAC region countries with full customs documentation.
road.faq3A: We operate 3.5-ton, 10-ton, and 30-ton trucks, plus flatbed and refrigerated vehicles for special cargo.
road.faq4Q: Is my cargo insured during road transport?
road.faq4A: Yes, all road freight is covered by basic transit insurance. Additional coverage is available for high-value goods.

warehousing.title: Warehousing
warehousing.subtitle: Secure storage and inventory management in Kribi and Douala
warehousing.description: Sea Bridge Logistics operates modern warehousing facilities in both Kribi and Douala, offering secure storage with advanced inventory management. Whether you need short-term storage during transit or long-term warehousing for distribution, our facilities are equipped with 24/7 security, climate control options, and digital inventory tracking.
warehousing.step1Desc: We evaluate your cargo type, volume, and storage duration to assign the right facility.
warehousing.step2Desc: Cargo is received, inspected, and logged into our digital inventory system.
warehousing.step3Desc: Goods are stored in appropriate conditions with 24/7 security and monitoring.
warehousing.step4Desc: When you're ready, we pick, pack, and dispatch your goods to the next destination.
warehousing.faq1A: We operate facilities in both Kribi (near the deep sea port) and Douala (in the Bonabéri industrial zone).
warehousing.faq2A: Yes, our bonded warehouse allows you to store imported goods before customs duties are paid, helping manage cash flow.
warehousing.faq3Q: Can I track my inventory online?
warehousing.faq3A: Yes, our digital inventory management system gives you real-time visibility of stock levels, movements, and storage conditions.
warehousing.faq4A: Rates depend on cargo type, volume, and duration. Contact us for a personalized quote — we offer competitive rates for long-term storage.

customs.title: Customs Clearance
customs.subtitle: Expert customs brokerage for smooth border crossings
customs.description: Navigating Cameroon's customs regulations can be complex. Sea Bridge Logistics provides professional customs brokerage services to ensure your goods clear customs quickly and compliantly. Our licensed customs agents handle all documentation, tariff classification, duty calculations, and liaison with customs authorities at both Kribi and Douala ports.
customs.step1Desc: We gather all required documents — commercial invoice, packing list, certificates of origin, etc.
customs.step2Desc: Our experts classify your goods under the correct HS codes to ensure accurate duty calculation.
customs.step3Desc: We submit the customs declaration electronically and liaise with authorities on your behalf.
customs.step4Desc: If physical inspection is required, we coordinate and facilitate the process for minimal delays.
customs.step5Desc: Once cleared, goods are released and we arrange immediate transport to your destination.
customs.faq1Q: What documents do I need for customs clearance?
customs.faq1A: Typically: commercial invoice, packing list, bill of lading/airway bill, certificate of origin, and any product-specific permits. We'll guide you through the requirements.
customs.faq2Q: How long does customs clearance take?
customs.faq2A: Standard clearance takes 2-5 business days. Our fast-track service can clear goods in 24-48 hours for urgent shipments.
customs.faq3Q: Can you handle restricted or regulated goods?
customs.faq3A: Yes, we have experience with regulated goods including food, pharmaceuticals, electronics, and chemicals. We ensure all permits and certifications are in order.
customs.faq4Q: Do you handle both import and export clearance?
customs.faq4A: Yes, we provide comprehensive customs brokerage for both imports into Cameroon and exports to international destinations.

supply.title: Supply Chain Management
supply.subtitle: End-to-end logistics from origin to final destination
supply.description: Sea Bridge Logistics offers comprehensive supply chain management services, coordinating every link from procurement to final delivery. We design and manage logistics solutions that optimize your entire supply chain — reducing costs, improving reliability, and giving you complete visibility. From raw materials to finished goods, we manage it all.
supply.step1Desc: We analyze your current supply chain to identify bottlenecks, inefficiencies, and cost-saving opportunities.
supply.step2Desc: Our team designs a tailored logistics strategy covering transport modes, routes, and timelines.
supply.step3Desc: We deploy the solution — integrating warehousing, transport, customs, and technology systems.
supply.step4Desc: Day-to-day management of your supply chain with real-time tracking and proactive issue resolution.
supply.step5Desc: Continuous improvement through data analysis, performance reviews, and strategy refinements.
supply.faq1A: We serve agriculture, mining, manufacturing, retail, FMCG, and construction industries across Central and West Africa.
supply.faq2Q: Can you manage international supply chains?
supply.faq2A: Yes, we coordinate multi-country supply chains spanning Africa, Europe, and Asia with local expertise at each touchpoint.
supply.faq3Q: Do you offer dedicated account managers?
supply.faq3A: Yes, every supply chain client gets a dedicated account manager who serves as your single point of contact.
supply.faq4Q: How do you measure supply chain performance?
supply.faq4A: We track KPIs including on-time delivery rate, cost per unit shipped, inventory accuracy, and transit time reliability — with regular reports.
